evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Count to my Cordova app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show the built-in counting UI.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCount integration code into EmptyApp.js, and shows the setup checklist (plugins to install, cordova prepare, camera permissions, CSP, minimum plugin version 7.6 for constructor pattern).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions cordova plugin add scandit-cordova-datacapture-core and scandit-cordova-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions cordova prepare after plugin install
Setup checklist mentions camera permissions are auto-configured by the plugins
document.addEventListener('deviceready' is used as the init gate
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
Scandit.BarcodeCount.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is called
Scandit.Camera.withSettings( is called
context.setFrameSource( is called
new Scandit.BarcodeCountSettings() is present
Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Scandit.Symbology.Code128 is enabled
new Scandit.BarcodeCount(settings) is present (context-free constructor used)
BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext( is NOT present
context.addMode(barcodeCount) is called after constructing BarcodeCount
Scandit.BarcodeCountView.forContextWithMode( or forContextWithModeAndStyle( is called
barcodeCountView.connectToElement( is called
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.On) is called to start the camera
The Scandit.* global is used (no 'import ... from scandit-cordova-datacapture-*' statements in runtime code)

evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Count to ProductListApp.js. When the scan phase ends, push each recognized barcode into the scannedProducts array and call renderProductList. Use EAN-13, EAN-8, and Code 128.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeCount integration to ProductListApp.js, wires a BarcodeCountListener.didScan callback that reads session.recognizedBarcodes and pushes each barcode into scannedProducts, and calls renderProductList. All existing state and render logic is preserved.

assertions.text: document.addEventListener('deviceready' is present
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
new Scandit.BarcodeCountSettings() is present
Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Scandit.Symbology.EAN8 is enabled
Scandit.Symbology.Code128 is enabled
new Scandit.BarcodeCount(settings) is present
context.addMode( is called
Scandit.BarcodeCountView.forContextWithMode( or forContextWithModeAndStyle( is called
barcodeCountView.connectToElement( is called
barcodeCount.addListener is called with a didScan callback
session.recognizedBarcodes is accessed inside the didScan callback
scannedProducts array is pushed into inside the didScan callback (semantic)
renderProductList() is called after updating scannedProducts (semantic)
Existing onClearTapped / clear button handler is preserved
camera.switchToDesiredState( is called to start the camera

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan Count app (IntegratedApp.js), hide the toolbar and the exit button. Show a torch control button in the top-right corner.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowToolbar = false, shouldShowExitButton = false, shouldShowTorchControl = true, and torchControlPosition = Scandit.Anchor.TopRight on the existing barcodeCountView.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.shouldShowToolbar = false is present
barcodeCountView.shouldShowExitButton = false is present
barcodeCountView.shouldShowTorchControl = true is present
barcodeCountView.torchControlPosition = Scandit.Anchor.TopRight is present (or TopRight anchor equivalent)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: Localize the scan hints in my Cordova MatrixScan Count app (IntegratedApp.js) to French: 'Appuyez pour scanner', 'Scan en cours…', 'Rapprochez-vous et rescannez', 'Éloignez-vous et rescannez'.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the four hint text properties on barcodeCountView to the provided French strings.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.textForTapShutterToScanHint is set to 'Appuyez pour scanner' or equivalent
barcodeCountView.textForScanningHint is set to 'Scan en cours…' or equivalent
barcodeCountView.textForMoveCloserAndRescanHint is set to 'Rapprochez-vous et rescannez' or equivalent
barcodeCountView.textForMoveFurtherAndRescanHint is set to 'Éloignez-vous et rescannez' or equivalent
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to scan items against an expected list in my Cordova MatrixScan Count app (IntegratedApp.js). The expected items are: barcode '12345670' (qty 1) and '98765432' (qty 2). When a barcode not in the list is tapped, log it to the console. When the full list is completed, show an alert.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a BarcodeCountCaptureList with TargetBarcode entries, calls barcodeCount.setBarcodeCountCaptureList, and wires view.listener with didTapRecognizedBarcodeNotInList and didCompleteCaptureList callbacks.

assertions.text: Scandit.BarcodeCountCaptureList.create( is called
Scandit.TargetBarcode.create('12345670', 1) is present
Scandit.TargetBarcode.create('98765432', 2) is present
barcodeCount.setBarcodeCountCaptureList( is called
view.listener or barcodeCountView.listener is assigned
didTapRecognizedBarcodeNotInList callback is implemented and logs the barcode data (semantic)
didCompleteCaptureList callback is implemented and shows an alert or completion message (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a stock-status overlay to my Cordova MatrixScan Count app (IntegratedApp.js). Show the status mode button. For each scanned barcode, call fetchStockStatus(barcode.data) (returns a Promise<'in_stock' | 'out_of_stock'>) and provide a BarcodeCountStatusProvider that reflects the result.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowStatusModeButton = true, calls view.setStatusProvider with a statusForBarcodes async callback that calls fetchStockStatus for each barcode, and maps the results to BarcodeCountStatusItem objects. Marks status API as beta in a comment.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.shouldShowStatusModeButton = true is present
barcodeCountView.setStatusProvider( is called
The statusForBarcodes callback is async or returns a Promise (semantic)
fetchStockStatus( is called inside the statusForBarcodes callback (semantic)
A BarcodeCountStatusItem or status result is returned for each barcode (semantic)
A comment notes that status mode is beta (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable tap-to-uncount in my Cordova MatrixScan Count app (IntegratedApp.js) so the user can deselect barcodes by tapping their highlight. Set the hint text to 'Tap to remove item'.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets tapToUncountEnabled = true and textForTapToUncountHint on barcodeCountView.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.tapToUncountEnabled = true is present
barcodeCountView.textForTapToUncountHint is set to 'Tap to remove item' or similar
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable the hardware trigger in my Cordova MatrixScan Count app (IntegratedApp.js). The user wants to use the volume button to trigger scanning. Check that the hardware trigger is supported before enabling it, and use the default key code.
evals.expected_output: The skill checks Scandit.BarcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerSupported, and if true calls view.enableHardwareTrigger(null) to use the default key code. On iOS, sets hardwareTriggerEnabled = true (or notes it reacts to the volume button on iOS regardless).

assertions.text: Scandit.BarcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerSupported is checked before enabling (semantic)
barcodeCountView.enableHardwareTrigger(null) is called OR barcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerEnabled = true is set
A comment or note explains Android API >=28 is required for hardware trigger (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView, camera) is preserved
